summary: Moved a large contiguous block of .sdata2 constants out of the monolithic asm/sdata2.s file and into the corresponding per-system assembly files through the range described as "up to ft_donkey1". The PR deleted 3236 lines from asm/sdata2.s and added .section .sdata2 blocks to 11 asm/melee files, with the largest destination being asm/melee/ft/code_8006DBF4.s. There were no PR body notes or review comments, so the intent is inferred from the title and diff: improve section ownership/locality while preserving existing labels and raw constant encodings.

key_files.role: largest destination for split .sdata2 constants; 2328 lines added
destination for mp .sdata2 constants; 306 lines added
destination for lb .sdata2 constants; 192 lines added
destination for cm .sdata2 constants, including labels lbl_804D7E00 through the excerpted lbl_804D7F08
destination for pl .sdata2 constants; 70 lines added

what_changed: Removed the existing large .sdata2 content from asm/sdata2.s in one hunk.
Appended .section .sdata2 blocks to per-module assembly files under asm/melee/cm, asm/melee/ef, asm/melee/ft, asm/melee/lb, asm/melee/mp, and asm/melee/pl.
Preserved raw global labels such as lbl_804D7E00, lbl_804D81D0, lbl_804D81F8, and lbl_804D8298 instead of renaming them.

Preserved constants as literal .4byte hex encodings, including common float and double encodings such as 0x3F800000, 0xBF800000, 0x43300000/0x80000000, and pi-related double halves like 0x3FF921FB/0x54442D18.
The PR changed only assembly/data layout files in the provided context; no C source or header edits are evidenced.
smart_moves: Kept labels and constant encodings stable while moving their physical location, which minimizes risk of changing references or generated binary layout.
Split .sdata2 by nearby object/module ownership rather than leaving a single global data blob, making future matching and per-file decompilation easier.
Appended each destination .sdata2 block after the existing contents of its assembly file, a simple tactic that avoids interleaving code edits with data movement.
Used exact .4byte representations rather than decimal float spellings, preserving bit identity for floats, doubles, sentinel values, and integer-looking constants.
decomp_lessons: When breaking up monolithic data sections, move constants into the assembly file that owns or references them while preserving label names and order.
For early-stage decomp work, splitting data sections can be valuable even before symbols are named because it localizes future C replacements and matching work.
Keep raw lbl_804Dxxxx labels until there is stronger evidence for semantic names; this PR provides no review/body evidence supporting semantic renames.
Double constants in .sdata2 often appear as two .4byte words; keep the pair intact and maintain alignment/padding exactly.
Large mechanical data relocations are easier to audit when the diff is a clear delete-from-central-file/add-to-destination-files transformation.
naming_conventions: Global data labels retained the existing address-derived form lbl_804Dxxxx.
Assembly destination files followed the existing path convention asm/melee/<system>/<object>.s, such as asm/melee/ft/code_8006DBF4.s and asm/melee/ef/efasync.s.
Unknown code files retained address-based names like code_80028B9C.s, code_80061D70.s, code_8006DBF4.s, code_8001E2F8.s, code_80041C78.s, and code_8003715C.s.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use explicit `.section .sdata2` blocks in each destination assembly file.
Represent constants with `.4byte` hex words to preserve exact IEEE-754 and integer bit patterns.
Keep `.global` directives immediately before each moved label.
Preserve paired words for double constants and conversion constants, for example 0x43300000 paired with 0x80000000 or 0x00000000.
Maintain zero padding words where present, often as a second `.4byte 0x00000000` after single-precision values in aligned slots.

review_feedback.detail: The PR context reports 0 issue comments, 0 review comments, 0 reviews, and no PR body text, so no reviewer intent or requested changes can be extracted.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue splitting asm/sdata2.s after the ft_donkey1 range.
Search for when lbl_804D8298 and nearby ft/code_8006DBF4.s .sdata2 labels receive semantic names.
Look for build-system or linker-script changes related to distributed .sdata2 sections in asm/melee files.
Compare this PR with other monolithic data-section split PRs for consistent placement and alignment tactics.
